title: Bee Removal in Woodmere | $350 – $550 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional bee removal services in Woodmere, Long Island. Safe, humane bee hive removal by licensed experts. Serving Woodmere homes & businesses. Call today for $350 – $550 service.
intro: Best Pest Control NYC provides safe and effective bee removal services throughout Woodmere, Long Island. Our licensed technicians specialize in humane bee relocation and hive removal from residential and commercial properties. With transparent pricing between $350 and $550, we protect your family and property while preserving these essential pollinators.
whyUs: Our Woodmere bee removal specialists combine years of local experience with humane removal techniques that prioritize both your safety and environmental responsibility. We offer same-day emergency service, licensed and insured technicians, and comprehensive follow-up to ensure bees don't return to your property.
localContext: Woodmere's beautiful tree-lined streets and well-maintained properties near Peninsula Boulevard and the Atlantic Beach Bridge area create ideal nesting conditions for honeybees and carpenter bees during warmer months. From the historic homes near Woodmere Academy to modern residences throughout the Five Towns region, we've helped countless Woodmere property owners safely resolve bee infestations.

faqs.question: How much does bee removal cost in Woodmere?
faqs.answer: Our bee removal services in Woodmere range from $350 to $550, depending on the location of the hive, colony size, and accessibility. Ground-level hives are typically less expensive, while second-story or attic removals may be at the higher end. We provide free estimates and transparent pricing before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove bees from my Woodmere property?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and emergency bee removal services throughout Woodmere and the Five Towns area. Most bee removals are completed within 2-4 hours, depending on hive size and location. We understand the urgency of bee infestations, especially during summer months when outdoor activities are common, and prioritize rapid response times.

faqs.question: Do you relocate bees or exterminate them?
faqs.answer: Whenever possible, we prioritize humane bee relocation over extermination, working with local beekeepers to rehome colonies. However, in cases involving aggressive Africanized bees, structural damage, or severe safety risks, extermination may be necessary. We'll discuss the best approach for your specific situation during our inspection.

faqs.question: What types of bees are common in Woodmere homes?
faqs.answer: Woodmere properties commonly experience honeybee swarms, carpenter bees that damage wooden structures, bumblebees in ground nests, and occasionally yellow jackets. The area's mature trees and traditional home construction make it particularly attractive to carpenter bees. Our technicians are trained to identify and safely remove all bee species common to Long Island.
